name: Snow Report 2026-03-12 16:32:40
report:
Good evening, Pico family,

The weather overnight made for some challenging conditions today, but thanks to the hard work from our patrol and operations teams we were able to keep things running and make the most of it.

Looking ahead, there’s some good news in the forecast. The system arriving tomorrow night has been upgraded to a Winter Weather Advisory, with 5–9 inches of snow expected by the time it wraps up. Winter isn’t done with us just yet, and that fresh snow will be a welcome boost to the base as we move deeper into the season.

Tomorrow brings a mix of sun and clouds with a high near 29 at the base and 22 at the top. Winds shift to the south through the afternoon, and snow is likely to move in late in the day with the potential for 1 to 2 inches of fresh snow.

Surface conditions will remain variable, with a mix of loose granular, machine-groomed snow, and a few scattered areas of hardpack and thin cover. That said, we’re still holding onto a solid base across the mountain. Groomed terrain will offer the most consistent turns today. Our grooming team will be resurfacing Forty Niner, Fools Gold, Gold Rush, Expressway, and more. Be sure to check the Lifts & Trails Report for the full rundown.

The Summit Express Quad, Golden Express Quad, Little Pico Triple, and Bonanza Carpet are all scheduled to spin at 9:00 a.m.

Grab your Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding from March 13, 2026 through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Today is your last chance to lock in the best available price of $379. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade anytime for just $199.

Have fun out there! 

 – Evan

 

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.   

Uphill Travel   

  • Pico Base to the top of Golden Express Quad - Open
  • Pico Base to Pico Summit - Open
  • Summit Glade Route - CLOSED
  • Killington/Pico Mountain Interconnect – Open  

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.              

Mountain Services   

  • Breakfast: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor) starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and at 8:00 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Menu includes egg sandwiches and our new signature breakfast burritos.
  • Lunch: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor). Menu includes our full grill menu of burgers, tenders, veggie burgers, and more, plus Caesar salads that are made to order.
  • Last Run Lounge (3rd floor): Open every day that lifts are spinning. Bar service is available from 11:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. Menu includes soups, garlic knots, and lite fare, with our pizza station opening up on weekends and busier days. Convenient QR code ordering is available.
  • Guest Services, Rentals, and Pico Sports Shop: Open every day that lifts are spinning from 30 minutes before lifts open until 30 minutes after lifts close.  

Tickets & Lessons   

  • Tickets are on sale at picomountain.com and must be purchased online. My Pico 2025/2026 Season Passes are still on sale!
  • Group lessons are available! Click here for more information. All lessons and rentals must also be booked online.
  • For information on cancellation and change policies, please visit picomountain.com/cancellation   

Pico Fitness Center                              

Visit picomountain.com/fitness to see our fitness class schedule for the winter, plus more details about memberships and pool lane reservations.      

Hours of Operation:   

Monday thru Friday: 6:00 a.m. - 8:00 p.m. 
Saturday & Sunday: 8:00 a.m. - 6:00 p.m.          

We strive to have the most up-to-date and accurate snow report, but errors can happen from time to time. If you have feedback or a correction for us, please share it here.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-12 06:42:37
report:
Good morning, Pico family,

We’re excited to welcome everyone back to the mountain today after the midweek break. It’s been a warm and wet stretch the past few days, but there’s still plenty of good skiing and riding to be had, especially on our snowmaking trails.

Early this morning we saw very light snow showers before tapering off, with cloudy skies and temperatures falling through the day. A west wind will keep things feeling brisk at times, with gusts reaching up to around 45 mph. Snowfall should remain minimal, though a light dusting isn’t out of the question.

Our ski patrol team has been assessing conditions, and a number of our trails are closed due to firm conditions caused by a refreeze. Our groomed terrain will offer the best skiing & riding today. 

After several days of warmer weather and unwelcome wet stuff, natural terrain has taken a bit of a hit, especially in the trees. The good news is that coverage on our snowmaking trails remains solid, and groomed runs will be the best place to make turns today. 

Our grooming team refreshed around a dozen trails overnight including Forty Niner, Easy Street, Fools Gold, Exhibition, and B Slope, setting up some nice corduroy to start the day. Head over to Fool’s Gold for a classic Pico cruiser or explore the rest of our groomed terrain. Be sure to check the Lifts & Trails Report for the full rundown.

The Summit Express Quad, Golden Express Quad, Little Pico Triple, and Bonanza Carpet are all scheduled to spin at 9:00 a.m.

Grab your Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding from March 13, 2026 through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Today is your last chance to lock in the best available price of $379. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade anytime for just $199.

And while this week has certainly felt like spring, there are signs that winter may not be finished with us just yet, with the potential for some fresh snow returning to the forecast this weekend.

We’re glad to have everyone back. See you out there!

 – Evan

 

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-11 16:35:59
report:
Good evening, Pico family,

It’s been really feeling like Spring around here and tomorrow should bring more sweet skiing & riding. The snow continues to soften up as we progress deeper into the season and even with scattered showers on the way tonight, surfaces should be soft and fun as things will likely clear up by the time we open. Snow surfaces should be carving beautifully, making for confident turns and fun runs. 

Tomorrow morning brings a slight chance of rain and snow showers early before tapering off, with cloudy skies, falling temps through the day, and a stout west wind that’ll keep things feeling brisk. Gusts could reach up to 45 mph, and while snowfall will be minimal, a light dusting is possible.

Our grooming team is resurfacing Forty Niner, Easy Street, Fools Gold, Exhibition, and B Slope just to name a few. Head over to Fools Gold for a classic Spring run, and there are many more great options to choose from.

Today is the last day to get your Spring Pass at the lowest price, which gives you unlimited skiing and riding from March 13, 2026 through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade anytime for just $199.

Enjoy these beautiful days while we have them. Around here we sometimes call this “false spring,” and there are hints that some more winter-like weather could roll back in later this week and into the weekend.

Get psyched to shred again! 

- Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-03-11 06:00:13
report:
Good morning, Pico family,

It was another beautiful day out on the hill yesterday. Sunshine returned in full force and the snow softened nicely through the afternoon, making for some classic spring skiing across the mountain. It was a great way to wrap up another fun stretch of turns together.

Snow surfaces followed the typical spring cycle, starting a little firmer in the morning before softening up once the sun got to work. By the afternoon, conditions were smooth and forgiving in many areas with a few slower spots here and there as temperatures climbed. Even with the seasonal transition underway, we’re still holding onto solid coverage and plenty of good snow across the mountain.

As a reminder, Pico will be closed to the public today, March 11, as we return to our regular midweek operating schedule. Lifts will be spinning on Wednesday, March 11th for a private event. We look forward to welcoming everyone back on Thursday for more spring turns.

It’s also a great time to start thinking about your Spring Pass, which gives you unlimited skiing and riding from March 13, 2026 through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade anytime for just $199.

Enjoy these beautiful days while we have them. Around here we sometimes call this “false spring,” and there are hints that some more winter-like weather could roll back in later this week and into the weekend.

We’ll see you Thursday!

- Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-03-09 05:46:08
report:
Good morning, Pico family,

We had another great day on the hill yesterday with soft skiing and riding under plenty of sunshine. We lucked out with pretty good conditions this weekend, about as nice a kickoff to the spring season as you could ask for.

Sunny skies are back today with temperatures climbing to the low-to-mid 40s. However, it’s going to be quite a breezy one with southwest winds gusting up to 50 mph at times. 

Snow conditions stayed soft and fun to carve yesterday, and when the sun came out it was textbook spring skiing, making for another really enjoyable one on the hill. A few spots were skiing a little slower than previous days, and natural terrain is starting to show some thin cover in places. That said, we’re still holding onto plenty of wall-to-wall runs and solid snow depths to keep the good turns going.

Our grooming team laid down cord across 18 trails overnight, with the exception of Knomes Knoll terrain as that lift won’t be spinning on Monday per usual. Head to Forty Niner and Fools Gold for a good time on classic groomers. Be sure to check the Lifts & Trails Report for the full rundown. 

The Summit Express Quad, Golden Express Quad, Little Pico Triple, and Bonanza Carpet are all scheduled to spin at 9:00 a.m. The Outpost Double remains closed as we look to source and repair a new drive component for the lift. 

It’s about time to think about getting your Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding from March 13, 2026, through the very last day of the season. Plus, your Spring Pass is also valid at Killington until it closes for the season

Have a fun day of shredding! 

– Josh

 

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-08 16:05:15
report:
Good evening, Pico family,

We had another great day on the hill with soft skiing and riding under plenty of sunshine. We lucked out with fantastic conditions this weekend, about as nice a kickoff to the spring season as you could ask for.

Sunny skies are back tomorrow with temperatures climbing to around 46°F at the top and 52°F at the peak. It’s going to be quite a windy one with southwest winds coming across the mountain gusting up to 55 mph at times. Expect a blustery day out there. 

Snow conditions stayed soft and fun to carve through today, making for another enjoyable one on the hill. A few spots were skiing a little slower than yesterday, and natural terrain is starting to show some thin cover in places. That said, we’re still holding onto plenty of wall-to-wall runs and solid snow depths to keep the good turns going.

Our grooming team is after the same plan with the exception of Knomes Knoll terrain as that lift won’t be spinning on Monday per usual. Head to Forty Niner and Fools Gold for a good time on classic groomers. Be sure to check the Lifts & Trails Report for the full rundown. 

The Summit Express Quad, Golden Express Quad, Little Pico Triple, and Bonanza Carpet are all scheduled to spin at 9:00 a.m. The Outpost Double remains closed as we look to source and repair a new drive component for the lift. 

It’s about time to think about getting your Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding from March 13, 2026, through the very last day of the season. Plus, your Spring Pass is also valid at Killington until it closes for the season

Have a fun day of shredding! 

– Evan

  • Breakfast: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor) starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and at 8:00 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Menu includes egg sandwiches and our new signature breakfast burritos.
  • Lunch: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor). Menu includes our full grill menu of burgers, tenders, veggie burgers, and more, plus Caesar salads that are made to order.
  • Last Run Lounge (3rd floor): Open every day that lifts are spinning. Bar service is available from 11:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. Menu includes soups, garlic knots, and lite fare, with our pizza station opening up on weekends and busier days. Convenient QR code ordering is available.
  • Guest Services, Rentals, and Pico Sports Shop: Open every day that lifts are spinning from 30 minutes before lifts open until 30 minutes after lifts close.  

Tickets & Lessons   

  • Tickets are on sale at picomountain.com and must be purchased online. My Pico 2025/2026 Season Passes are still on sale!
  • Group lessons are available! Click here for more information. All lessons and rentals must also be booked online.
  • For information on cancellation and change policies, please visit picomountain.com/cancellation   

Pico Fitness Center                              

Visit picomountain.com/fitness to see our fitness class schedule for the winter, plus more details about memberships and pool lane reservations.      

Hours of Operation:   

Monday thru Friday: 6:00 a.m. - 8:00 p.m. 
Saturday & Sunday: 8:00 a.m. - 6:00 p.m.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-08 05:55:44
report:
Good morning, Pico family,

We had an amazing day of skiing and riding here yesterday. The sun popped out for much of the day, softening things up and making for some really fun, carve-friendly turns. It’s really comfortable on the slopes and we’re pretty excited to be making the most of it with every trail to choose from. 

Cloudy skies will start the day before gradually clearing by late morning, with temperatures falling to around the mid-40s by the afternoon. Expect a windy day on the mountain, with west winds of 29 to 38 mph and gusts keeping things feeling a little brisk.

The slopes skied and rode really well yesterday, with light, soft snow that was easy to edge into and was moving quickly. With conditions like this, it’s safe to say spring is starting to make its way onto the hill. The bumps were a joy on Upper Sunset 71 and Birch Glades and we can expect more of that today as the air stays warm and the snow stays soft across the mountain. 

Our grooming team stayed off any trails that didn’t see snowmaking to help preserve the depths there. Runs that are smooth and will provide a great ride are Mid and Lower Pike, Expressway, A Slope, and more. Be sure to check the Lifts & Trails Report for the full rundown. 

The Summit Express Quad, Golden Express Quad, Little Pico Triple, and Bonanza Carpet are all scheduled to spin at 8:30 a.m. The Knomes Knoll Triple will join at 9:00 a.m. The Outpost Double remains closed as we look to source and repair a new drive component for the lift. 

It’s about time to think about getting your Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding from March 13, 2026, through the very last day of the season. Plus, your Spring Pass is also valid at Killington until it closes for the season

Have a fun day of shredding! 

– Evan

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.   

Uphill Travel   

  • Pico Base to the top of Golden Express Quad - Open
  • Pico Base to Pico Summit - Open
  • Summit Glade Route - Open
  • Killington/Pico Mountain Interconnect – Open  

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.              

Mountain Services   

  • Breakfast: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor) starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and at 8:00 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Menu includes egg sandwiches and our new signature breakfast burritos.
  • Lunch: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor). Menu includes our full grill menu of burgers, tenders, veggie burgers, and more, plus Caesar salads that are made to order.
  • Last Run Lounge (3rd floor): Open every day that lifts are spinning. Bar service is available from 11:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. Menu includes soups, garlic knots, and lite fare, with our pizza station opening up on weekends and busier days. Convenient QR code ordering is available.
  • Guest Services, Rentals, and Pico Sports Shop: Open every day that lifts are spinning from 30 minutes before lifts open until 30 minutes after lifts close.  

Tickets & Lessons   

  • Tickets are on sale at picomountain.com and must be purchased online. My Pico 2025/2026 Season Passes are still on sale!
  • Group lessons are available! Click here for more information. All lessons and rentals must also be booked online.
  • For information on cancellation and change policies, please visit picomountain.com/cancellation   

Pico Fitness Center                              

Visit picomountain.com/fitness to see our fitness class schedule for the winter, plus more details about memberships and pool lane reservations.

Saturday & Sunday: 8:00 a.m. - 6:00 p.m.
